NVIDIAがGPU Display Driver向けの大規模セキュリティ更新を公開した。Linux版ではCVSS 8.8のuse-after-free脆弱性CVE-2026-24187が修正され、権限昇格や任意コード実行につながる危険があった。  CVE-2026-24187はLinux向けDisplay Driverに存在し、ローカル攻撃者がuse-after-free状態を引き起こせる。悪用されるとDoS、情報漏えい、データ改ざん、権限昇格、任意コード実行が可能になる。  さらにWindows・Linux両方でCVSS 7.8の複数欠陥も修正された。CVE-2026-24190ではGPUリソースへの不正アクセスが可能で、CVE-2026-24191はWindowsドライバのTOCTOU問題、CVE-2026-24192はLinuxドライバの整数変換不備によるヒープバッファオーバーフロー、CVE-2026-24193は境界外書き込み問題に該当する。  加えてvGPU SoftwareやCloud Gaming環境でもCVE-2026-24200などの重大欠陥が修正された。GPU仮想化マネージャ内のスタックメモリuse-after-freeで、コード実行や権限昇格の恐れがある。影響はVMware vSphere、XenServer、RHEL KVM、Ubuntu、Windows Serverなど広範囲に及ぶ。  対象はGeForce、RTX、Quadro、Teslaを含む多数製品で、管理者にはR595、R590、R580、R570、R535系ドライバの即時更新が推奨されている。 securityonline.info/nvidia-g…

21-year-old RCE vulnerability in FreeBSD (CVE-2026-42511)  PT ID: PT-2026-36009  The article describes the critical vulnerability CVE-2026-42511 -> (dbugs.ptsecurity.com/vulnera…) in the FreeBSD DHCP client ("dhclient"), which existed for more than 20 years and allowed arbitrary code execution with "root" privileges. The issue lies in the fact that the BOOTP "file" field is written into the lease file without proper escaping; later, during reprocessing, this file is interpreted as a configuration and passed to "dhclient-script", where it can be executed as malicious code.  An attacker only needs to control the DHCP server on the same network (for example, via a rogue Wi-Fi access point or DHCP response spoofing) to inject malicious instructions and achieve full system compromise.  📎 Article: aisle.com/blog/aisle-discove… #dbugs_attacks

Five security stories landed this week, and four turned on the same hinge: an admin-grade credential ended up somewhere it was not meant to be.  Echo Protocol leaked an admin key on Monad before the team burned the residual mint.  Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20182 lets an unauthenticated attacker take over an internal high-privileged account on the controller.  Mini Shai-Hulud pushed validly-signed malware to antv after stealing a maintainer's npm token.  MiniPlasma turns any local user into SYSTEM via the Cloud Files driver on fully patched Windows 11.  In every case the credential was engineered for a single human and ended up inside a process, a packet, or a public registry.  DLTA tracks admin-access exposure as one intelligence stream across all four stacks.

Windows 취약점 폭증과 금융권 API 위기: 동맹 밖의 기업이 생존하는 법 📷 5월 3주차 보안 경보: 무너지는 인프라 신뢰성   2026년 5월 18일 현재, 글로벌 사이버 보안의 전쟁터는 그 어느 때보다 뜨겁습니다.  미국 CISA가 윈도우 핵심 취약점의 실제 악용을 경고하고, 글로벌 서버 공급망까지 흔들리는 지금, 특히 금융권과 엔터프라이즈 인프라를 타깃으로 한 AI해킹은 인간 방어자의 속도를 완전히 초월했습니다.  글로벌 보안 동맹 '글래스윙'이 그들만의 실시간 패치 성벽을 쌓는 동안, 동맹 밖에 놓인 대한민국 기업들은 어떤 무기로 이 거대한 위협에 맞서야 할까요?  1. AI가 주도하는 '익스플로잇 체인'의 공포 최근 CISA가 경고한 Windows MSHTML 취약점(CVE-2024-30051) 사례는 해커들이 AI를 어떻게 활용하는지 적나라하게 보여줍니다. - 취약점의 결합: 해커는 단 하나의 강력한 제로데이를 찾지 않습니다. 영리한 AI 에이전트가 방치된 구형 취약점 3~4개를 엮어 순식간에 시스템 최고 권한(Admin)을 탈취하는 '체인형 공격'을 감행합니다. - API 비즈니스 로직 타깃: 금융권 API의 허점을 노린 자율형 봇넷은 탐지 시스템을 우회하여 계정을 탈취하고 한도를 우회하는 등, 즉각적인 재무적 손실을 입히고 있습니다.  2. 거대 클라우드 AI의 치명적 한계와 '미토스'의 그림자 많은 기업이 보안 진단을 위해 외부 클라우드 AI 도구를 검토하지만, 금융권과 공공기관에서는 태생적으로 도입이 불가능합니다. - 소스코드 유출: 기업의 심장부인 소스코드와 API 명세서를 외부 클라우드로 전송하는 순간, 그 자체가 또 다른 공급망 유출의 통로가 됩니다. - 통제 불가능한 비용: 수천만 건의 자산을 종량제 토큰 비용을 내며 클라우드로 스캔하는 것은 예산의 재앙입니다.  3. 독자적 자강(自强)의 유일한 해답, 'MOMUS AI' 디포트리스가 개발한 MOMUS AI는 클라우드 연결을 완전히 끊은 100% 온프레미스 기반 자율 모의해킹 시스템입니다. 미토스(Mythos)급 AI 공격에 맞설 수 있는 국내 유일의 독자적 파수꾼입니다. - ReAct 자율 추론 엔진: 단순한 시그니처 대조 스캐너가 아닙니다. AI가 스스로 관찰하고, 추론하고, 우회 공격 계획을 짜며 우리 시스템의 '익스플로잇 체인'을 먼저 찾아내 부숩니다. - 데이터 유출 Zero (Local Air-Gap): 듀얼 GTX 3090 온프레미스 인프라를 기반으로, 외부망과의 연결 없이 전산실 내부에서만 완벽한 데이터 주권을 유지하며 구동됩니다. - C-Level 재무 리스크 리포트: 취약점을 방치했을 때 발생할 개인정보보호법(PIPA)상의 매출액 10% 과징금 리스크를 재무적 수치(₩)로 환산하여 경영진의 합리적 의사결정을 지원합니다.  4. 최후의 보루: "핵심 데이터는 '금고' 속에 숨겨라" Windows가 장악당하고 서버 펌웨어가 오염되는 최악의 상황, 해커가 관리자 권한으로 소프트웨어 스냅샷을 백업까지 통째로 지우는 순간 기업은 파산합니다.  이때 기업을 구원하는 것은 오직 디포트리스 물리 격리 금고뿐입니다. 네트워크와 물리적으로 완전히 단절된 에어갭(Air-Gap) 상태에서, 개정된 국가 망 보안체계(N2SF)를 준수하는 일방향 데이터 다이오드 전송을 통해 그 어떤 AI 에이전트도 건드릴 수 없는 절대적 불변 백업을 완성합니다.  📷 결 론 사고는 예고 없이 찾아오지만, 기업의 파멸은 막을 수 있습니다. 글로벌 보안 동맹의 정보 소외를 걱정할 시간에, MOMUS AI로 우리 시스템을 먼저 철저하게 털어보고, 핵심 자산은 디포트리스 물리 금고에 숨기십시오.  AI로 공격하는 시대, 방어자 역시 대등한 수준의 AI 무기와 강력한 물리적 방패를 갖춰야만 살아남을 수 있습니다. #디포트리스 #MOMUSAI #Windows취약점 #API보안 #금융권보안 #AI해킹 #미토스 #제로트러스트 #물리격리백업 #사이버레질리언스

Shai-Huludワームのソースコード流出から数日で模倣マルウェアがnpmへ出現した。攻撃者は改変版を悪性パッケージとして公開し、開発者の認証情報窃取やDDoSボット感染を既に開始している。  Shai-Huludは2025年から続くオープンソース供給網攻撃で使われたワームで、npmメンテナーの認証情報やAPIキーを盗み、侵害アカウント経由で悪性アップデートを拡散していた。後にTeamPCPとの関連が判明し、GitHubへソースコードが一時公開されたことで状況が急変した。  Ox Securityによると、既に少なくとも1人の攻撃者が流出コードを再利用し、「chalk-tempalte」など4件の悪性npmパッケージを公開している。中でも「chalk-tempalte」はShai-Huludをほぼそのまま移植したもので、盗んだ認証情報をGitHubリポジトリへ送信する挙動も確認された。  さらに「axois-utils」などはAxios関連ライブラリを装ったタイポスクワッティング型で、開発者を誤導してインストールさせる狙いがある。一部パッケージは認証情報窃取だけでなく、感染端末をDDoSボットネットへ組み込む機能まで備えていた。  4件のパッケージは発見前に週2600回以上ダウンロードされており、研究者は「今後さらに模倣型サプライチェーン攻撃が急増する」と警告している。  securityaffairs.com/192366/m…

sample_tweets.text: 기술 민족주의와 국가 안보'  앤트로픽 미토스의 사이버 위협 공유 네트워크 구축 소식은 빅테크와 정부가 왜 AI 독점에 사활을 거는지 보여주는 완벽한 사례입니다. 📉⚔️  자동화된 대규모 위협:  AI가 소프트웨어 취약점을 찾는 속도가 인간 보안 전문가의 패치 속도를 압도하면서, 금융·방산 등 국가 핵심 인프라가 상시적인 마비 위협에 노출되었습니다.  통제되는 기술, 소외되는 동맹:  미국 중심의 소수 기업(아마존, 마이크로소프트, JP모건 등)만 이 강력한 방패를 공유받고 있으며, 미 정부는 기술 유출을 막고 있습니다.  결국 AI 패권국이 '엠바고'를 걸면 타국은 사이버 방어의 사각지대에 놓이게 됩니다. 대한민국을 비롯한 비미국권 국가들이 독자적인 파운데이션 모델(독파모)과 소버린 AI 인프라를 구축해야 하는 진짜 이유는 여기에 있습니다. 기술 자립 없는 안보는 신기루일 뿐입니다. 🇰🇷🪖  wsj.com/tech/ai/anthropic-le… #소버린AI #앤트로픽 #ClaudeMythos #CyberSecurity #국가안보 #AI패권 #기술자립

"파리 G7 재무장관, 중앙은행 총재 회의 핵심 요약"  '성장 둔화와 에너지 충격이 동시에 오는 상황에서 어떻게 글로벌 시스템을 안정시킬 것인가'가 메인 주제  가장 중요하게 논의된 건 크게 3가지 1. 글로벌 불균형 2. 중동발 에너지 리스크 3. 금융, 재정 안정 대응  + AI 사이버 위협, 희토류 공급망  첫 번째로 G7은 세계 경제의 불균형 문제를 장기적인 시스템 리스크로 바라봤다  경상수지 격차, 성장의 편중, 국가 간 정책 공조 약화가 이어지면서 같은 글로벌 시스템 안에서도 각국의 속도와 방향이 점점 달라지고 있다는 것이다  그래서 회의에서는 개발재원 확대, 다자개발은행 역할 강화, 취약국 지원 같은 자금 조달 메커니즘도 함께 논의됐다  두 번째 핵심은 에너지  중동 긴장으로 유가 변동성이 커지자 G7은 에너지 공급망 안정과 시장 충격 완화에 초점을 맞췄다  미국 역시 러시아산 원유 제재 일부 예외를 연장하며 취약국 원유 접근성과 시장 안정 사이의 균형을 맞추려는 모습을 보였다  세 번째는 금융, 재정 안정 대응  중앙은행들은 성급한 방향 전환보다 “데이터를 보며 판단하겠다”는 입장을 유지했다  이건 유가 상승이 다시 인플레이션을 자극할 가능성과 그에 따른 금리 불확실성을 경계하고 있다는 것이다  이번 G7 회의는 에너지, 물가, 금리, 유동성, 지정학 리스크가 하나의 시스템처럼 연결되어 있다는 점을 다시 한 번 보여줬다  그래서 투자할 때에도 이 전체 시스템을 이해할 수 있어야 한다

If your team won't deploy on a Friday afternoon, that's worth paying attention to.  Friday deploy freezes exist for a reason. Usually a specific incident that nobody wants to repeat. The freeze is a rational response.  But it's also a signal. And if Friday afternoon is too dangerous to deploy, the question is what makes it dangerous, and whether that condition only exists on Fridays.  It usually doesn't.  Deployment anxiety traces back to the same few things. Manual steps that depend on someone doing them correctly under pressure. Environments that behave differently from production. No automated rollback. Limited visibility in the minutes after the deploy goes out.  None of these are Friday problems. They're process problems that feel more exposed on a Friday because the safety net of a full team isn't there.  The teams that deploy on Fridays without a second thought haven't lowered their standards. They've removed the conditions that made deploying feel risky. Automated pipelines. Consistent environments. Rollbacks that take seconds. Observability that tells you quickly whether the deploy needs reversing.  Deployment should be boring. Not brave. At Salus that is our goal, make deployments easy, and might I even say, boring.  Book a demo below.  salus.cloud/talk-to-sales

𝐉𝐎𝐇𝐍𝐒 𝐂𝐎𝐔𝐍𝐓𝐘 𝐈𝐍𝐌𝐀𝐓𝐄 𝐂𝐇𝐀𝐑𝐆𝐄𝐃; 𝐖𝐎𝐌𝐀𝐍 𝐀𝐑𝐑𝐄𝐒𝐓𝐄𝐃 𝐅𝐎𝐑 𝐄𝐗𝐄𝐂𝐔𝐓𝐈𝐍𝐆 𝐀 𝐂𝐎𝐍𝐓𝐑𝐀𝐁𝐀𝐍𝐃 𝐃𝐑𝐎𝐏 𝐎𝐍 𝐑𝐄𝐒𝐓𝐑𝐈𝐂𝐓𝐄𝐃 𝐋𝐀𝐖 𝐄𝐍𝐅𝐎𝐑𝐂𝐄𝐌𝐄𝐍𝐓 𝐏𝐑𝐎𝐏𝐄𝐑𝐓𝐘  On Monday, April 13th, SJSO Security Threat Intelligence Specialists, conducting reviews of remote video visits, learned of a conversation between inmate, Devin Delaney, and his girlfriend, Jacqueline Mottor, planning to execute a contraband drop on restricted law enforcement property.  Through investigative efforts, detectives determined Delaney asked Mottor to purchase nicotine vapes and pouches and drop them off on SJSO property for him to collect during his work squad assignment.  Mottor agreed and on Sunday, April 12, was seen on SJSO surveillance cameras trespassing in a law enforcement restricted area to hide the contraband.   Deputies located Delaney with fourteen nicotine pouches in his possession, and one nicotine vape was located in a porta potty on SJSO property. Press Invite  Is Speaking for the Workers’ Rights a threat to National Security?  Press Conference against the slapping of NSA charges on senior journalist Satyam Verma and student activist Akriti. and against the UP Police’s ongoing witch-hunt against activists, journalists and workers related to the Noida workers’ protest!  Date: 20 May 2026 Time: 3 PM Venue: Press Club of India, Delhi  More than a month has passed since the UP Police unlawfully arrested seven activists and hundreds of workers for their alleged role in inciting the violence and arson that broke out during the workers’ strike in Noida on 13 April 2026. While serious questions have been raised by multiple news reports, workers’ testimonials and independent journalists’ findings on the role of UP Police in incitement of violence, no inquiry has taken place on the same, and instead,  a witch-hunt of activists and workers has been carried out.   After a series of unlawful arrests, raids, custodial torture and fabrication of evidence, after failing to produce a single shred of real evidence against any of the accused in numerous hearings in the courts, the UP Police resorted to invoking the draconian National Security Act, 1980 against two of the accused, Satyam Verma, a senior journalist, and public intellectual, and Akriti Chaudhary, a DU graduate, theatre artist and student activist. It is a brazen move to keep the accused from getting bail for an extended period of time. The baselessness of these charges will be discussed in the press conference by advocates and activists. The document stating the grounds of arrest under NSA is a laughable one, with completely false claims, lack of concrete evidence, and concocted conspiracies.   Moreover, during this entire period, the UP Police has orchestrated a vicious media trial of the activists and workers. Without producing any evidence in court, the UP Police has released numerous press releases with fabricated narratives, framing the accused as having “Pakistan links”, being “urban naxals”, having “Nepal ties”, as criminals and terrorists who “have left ideology” while the investigation is not even complete! In fact, the Supreme Court in a hearing of petition against custodial torture of the activists, strongly reprimanded the UP Police for branding workers and activists as “terrorists”, and observed that having “left ideology” is not a crime in this country. The Division Bench of the SC ordered the UP Police to produce the accused Aditya Anand and Rupesh Roy before the apex court. The magistrate of the Sessions Court has also ordered a DCP inquiry into the custodial torture of Aditya Anand.   Instead of following due procedure and being responsible in its interactions with the media, the UP Police has instead taken the route of persecuting the journalists, forums, and social media pages that are covering the real facts of the case. In the last few days, it has come to light that the UP Police has filed FIRs against social media pages that have been reporting on the case, conducting a brazen attack on the freedom of press itself! It has in fact claimed that these pages have raised questions on the conduct of UP Police, and have created a provocative environment by doing so. Since when has raising questions become a crime? And if merely reporting the truth is enough to disturb peace, then it begs the question: was this peace or silence imposed by repression and misinformation?  We urge all conscientious media persons and outlets to attend and cover this pertinent press conference, because what is taking place today is not just an attack on a few activists and workers. It is an attack on the freedom of press itself, it is an attack on the most fundamental right to freedom of expression, and an attack on the right of all workers to raise their just demands and resist oppression and exploitation.   The press conference will be addressed by activists, intellectuals, and advocates, friends and relatives of the accused.  •⁠  ⁠Campaign for the Release of Workers and Activists of Noida (CaRWAN)

sample_tweets.text: 🚨A data broker marketplace listing published on May 18, 2026, claims to offer 3.3 million customer registration records belonging to Deutsche Telekom, Germany’s largest telecommunications provider. The post advertises a comprehensive database export containing highly sensitive personally identifiable information (PII), financial details, and contract data, with escrow services accepted for transactions.  According to the listing, the dataset includes fields such as full names, dates of birth, national identification numbers, email addresses, phone numbers, physical addresses, and International Bank Account Numbers (IBANs). The accompanying JSON sample reveals structured customer profiles with detailed contract specifics, including tariff types, monthly pricing, subscription start and end dates, and banking connection details. The seller directs potential buyers to a Telegram channel for samples, pricing, and negotiation.  Deutsche Telekom AG operates one of the world’s largest telecom networks, serving over 300 million customers globally and employing approximately 250,000 people. As a designated critical infrastructure operator in Germany, any compromise of its customer database carries significant implications for data privacy, financial security, and regulatory compliance.  While the marketplace post asserts the authenticity and volume of the leak, independent verification remains pending. Data broker listings frequently circulate on underground forums, sometimes featuring outdated, partially fabricated, or aggregated records. Cybersecurity analysts recommend treating such listings as high-risk indicators, particularly given the inclusion of active IBANs and national ID numbers, which could facilitate identity theft, account takeover attacks, and targeted phishing campaigns.  The exposure of financial account details alongside comprehensive PII elevates the threat level substantially. Security experts advise Deutsche Telekom to investigate potential internal access logs, third-party vendor compromises, or API misconfigurations that may have facilitated the data exfiltration. Customers are encouraged to monitor bank statements, review credit reports, and consider enhanced identity protection measures while the incident is under review.  #CyberSecurity #DataBreach #ThreatIntelligence #DarkWeb #TelecomSecurity #IdentityTheft

🚨A hacker collective operating under the name "EsqueleSquad" has claimed responsibility for a large-scale data breach affecting multiple major Argentine government, financial, and healthcare institutions. According to a public forum post and associated Telegram channel, the group alleges it has obtained and is releasing over 33 million records containing highly sensitive personal, financial, and medical data.  The alleged leak primarily targets three entities: the Banco Central de la República Argentina (BCRA), the Instituto de Obra Social Municipal de la Ciudad de Buenos Aires (IOMA), and the Policía Federal Argentina (PFA, now restructured as the Policía de la Nación Argentina). Additionally, the group claims to possess a private dossier on Axel Kicillof, the Governor of Buenos Aires Province.  According to the breach details, the BCRA dataset contains approximately 32 million credit scoring records. Sample data provided by the group includes national identification numbers (DNI), tax IDs (CUIT), birth dates, and detailed debt information linked to major Argentine financial institutions such as Banco Santander Argentina and Banco de la Provincia de Buenos Aires.  The IOMA dataset reportedly includes over 1 million affiliate records. The exposed data features full names, residential addresses, membership numbers, email addresses, and highly sensitive medical information, including diagnosed chronic conditions (e.g., diabetes, hypercholesterolemia) and prescribed medications (e.g., atorvastatin, bisoprolol). IOMA serves as the municipal social security and healthcare provider for Buenos Aires City, covering millions of residents.  Furthermore, the group claims to have leaked 903 classified PDF documents from the national police force, alongside a personal dossier on Governor Kicillof containing phone numbers, chat logs, residential addresses, and credit scoring data.  "EsqueleSquad" states that the data is being released for free via Telegram and a third-party file-hosting service, with promises of additional dumps if the thread receives community support. As of now, neither the BCRA, IOMA, nor the Argentine government has issued an official confirmation or incident report regarding the breach. Cybersecurity professionals typically advise treating such dark web and forum claims with caution until independent verification or official disclosure occurs. However, the scale and sensitivity of the alleged exposure—spanning national financial infrastructure, public healthcare, and law enforcement—would represent a critical cybersecurity incident if verified.  If confirmed, the breach would directly impact millions of Argentine citizens, exposing their financial standing, medical histories, and personal identifiers. Authorities and affected institutions are expected to monitor the situation closely and may initiate forensic investigations, credit monitoring alerts, and data protection reviews in accordance with Argentina's Personal Data Protection Law.  #CyberSecurity #DataBreach #Argentina #ThreatIntelligence #CyberThreats #DarkWeb #DataLeak #HealthcareSecurity #FinancialSecurity #GovernmentBreach #CTI #ThreatMon #InfoSec #CyberAttack #IdentityTheft #DigitalRisk #BCRA #IOMA #PFA

🇧🇷 A threat actor is advertising an alleged database containing more than 557,000 Vivo Brazil customer account records, claiming the dataset includes authentication and contact-related information.  According to the post, the exposed records allegedly contain: • Email addresses • Phone numbers • Passwords  If verified, this type of dataset could present substantial risks due to the role telecom providers play as identity and authentication hubs for users.  Telecommunications providers remain prime targets because compromised customer data can enable: • SIM swap attacks • MFA/OTP interception • Account takeover attempts • Credential stuffing campaigns • Social engineering operations • Identity fraud and financial abuse  The exposure of passwords is particularly concerning if: • Passwords were stored insecurely • Users reused credentials across services • Attackers combine the dataset with other leaked records  Brazil continues to experience elevated underground market activity involving: • CPF-linked identity datasets • Telecom customer records • Banking fraud ecosystems • Mobile-focused credential abuse  Potential downstream risks include: • WhatsApp hijacking • Banking account compromise • Mobile carrier impersonation scams • Phishing campaigns targeting Vivo customers  Organizations in the telecom sector should prioritize: • MFA enforcement • Password hashing reviews • Credential exposure monitoring • SIM swap detection mechanisms • Customer anomaly detection • Dark web monitoring and threat intelligence integration  Users are strongly advised to: • Reset passwords immediately • Avoid password reuse • Enable MFA where possible • Monitor telecom and banking accounts for suspicious activity  At this time, the authenticity and origin of the alleged dataset remain unverified.  #DDW #Intelligence #Brazil #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb #DataBreach #ThreatIntelligence #InfoSec

🇷🇺 A threat actor has claimed to leak an alleged internal database belonging to Perm National Research Polytechnic University (PSTU / ПНИПУ), one of Russia’s major technical universities.  According to the post, the compromised system is described as a student research accounting and monitoring platform associated with: “uchetnirs.bf.pstu.ru”  The actor claims the exposed database contains: • ~362,786 rows of data • UTF-8/CSV formatted tables • Student and research-related records • Internal academic monitoring information • Contact and organizational data  Based on the visible samples, the exposed records may include: • Names and surnames • Email addresses • Telephone numbers • Group/class identifiers • Department/faculty references • Student academic information • Internal tracking identifiers  Educational and research institutions remain frequent targets due to: • Legacy infrastructure • Large user populations • Decentralized IT environments • Limited segmentation between academic systems • High-value research and identity data  Potential risks associated with these exposures include: • Student identity theft • Credential stuffing attacks • Spear-phishing targeting students and faculty • Academic fraud • Research-related espionage • Long-term reputational damage  This incident also highlights the broader trend of: • Academic institution targeting • Public release of university datasets on underground forums • Threat actors leveraging CSV/structured database dumps for credibility  Organizations in the education and research sector should prioritize: • External exposure management • Database access auditing • MFA enforcement • Monitoring for leaked credentials • Segmentation of academic and administrative systems • Incident response preparedness  At this time, the authenticity and full scope of the alleged leak remain unverified.  #DDW #Intelligence #Russia #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb #DataBreach #ThreatIntelligence #InfoSec

🎮 A threat actor is advertising an alleged database containing 500,000 PlayStation-related logs, claiming the dataset includes account and authentication metadata associated with PlayStation Network (PSN) users.  According to the post, the leaked records allegedly contain: • Usernames • Passwords/log credentials • Account types • Account creation dates • Public profile information • Service provider details • Account status metadata • Verification-related fields • Login timestamps • URLs and account references  While the authenticity of the dataset remains unverified, the structure strongly resembles: • Infostealer log collections • Credential aggregation datasets • Compiled combo lists from previous infections • Account session/token harvesting operations  Important context: There is currently no evidence suggesting a direct compromise of Sony or PlayStation infrastructure itself. In many similar cases, “PlayStation logs” are actually harvested from infected user devices through infostealer malware rather than breaches of the platform provider.  Potential risks for affected users include: • Account takeover attempts • Credential stuffing attacks • Financial fraud tied to stored payment methods • Unauthorized purchases • Social engineering targeting gamers and streamers • Resale of gaming accounts on underground forums  Gaming ecosystems continue to be heavily targeted because they often contain: • Saved payment methods • Digital assets/in-game inventories • Linked email accounts • Loyalty/subscription services • Young or less security-aware users  Users should immediately: • Reset passwords • Enable MFA/2FA • Review account login history • Remove unused payment methods • Avoid password reuse across services • Scan systems for infostealer malware  At this time, the legitimacy, origin, and freshness of the alleged dataset remain unverified.  #DDW #Intelligence #USA #Japan #PlayStation #PSN #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb #DataBreach #ThreatIntelligence #InfoSec

According to the post, the exposed data allegedly contains: • 399K customer records • 780K booking/reservation entries • 42K driver-related records • 190K standalone CPF entries • 88K payment token/card-related records • Admin and partner portal accounts allegedly stored with plaintext passwords  The threat actor claims the dataset includes: • Full names • CPF numbers • Email addresses • Phone numbers • Home addresses • CNH/passport information • Reservation history and rental details • Partial payment/card metadata  If verified, this would represent a significant privacy and fraud risk for affected individuals due to the sensitivity of Brazilian identity data and travel-related records.  Key concerns include: • Identity theft and CPF abuse • Credential stuffing attacks • Travel pattern intelligence exposure • Financial fraud attempts • Social engineering against customers and partners • Potential compromise of administrative portals  Travel, rental, and mobility platforms continue to be high-value targets because they aggregate: • Identity documents • Payment data • Geolocation/travel history • Corporate accounts • Reservation metadata • Customer support records  Organizations operating in this sector should prioritize: • Password hashing audits • MFA enforcement for admin portals • Tokenization and payment data segregation • Continuous dark web monitoring • Third-party access reviews • Exposure validation and incident response readiness  At this time, the authenticity and full scope of the alleged dataset remain unverified.  #DDW #Intelligence #Brazil #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb #DataBreach #ThreatIntelligence #InfoSec

🇮🇩 A threat actor has claimed responsibility for compromising more than 20 Indonesian SILSP/LSP-related databases, allegedly linked to certification and professional training institutions across the country.  The actor claims the campaign is ongoing and appears to be using extortion tactics, threatening continued publication of data and additional attacks unless ransom demands are met.  According to the post, the exposed data may involve: • Professional certification records   • Institutional databases   • Employee and administrative information   • Student or participant-related records   • Internal operational data    The actor also publicly named individuals allegedly associated with the targeted institutions — a tactic commonly used in pressure-based extortion campaigns to amplify reputational damage.  Key observations: • Multi-victim targeting pattern suggests opportunistic sector-wide activity   • Educational and certification institutions remain attractive due to weaker cyber hygiene and limited security budgets   • Public pressure/extortion narratives are increasingly being used instead of silent negotiations   • Threat actors are attempting to establish credibility by continuously releasing updates and “proof” samples    This incident highlights a growing trend where cybercriminal groups target: • Universities   • Certification bodies   • Vocational training organizations   • Government-linked educational systems   • Professional licensing platforms    Beyond ransomware and data theft, these campaigns can lead to: • Identity fraud   • Credential abuse   • Phishing operations   • Supply-chain compromise opportunities   • Long-term reputational damage    Organizations handling educational, certification, and citizen-related records should prioritize: • External attack surface monitoring   • Credential exposure detection   • Database exposure auditing   • MFA enforcement   • Backup isolation   • Incident response readiness   • Third-party/vendor security reviews    At this time, the authenticity and full scope of the alleged breach remain unverified.  #DDW #Intelligence #Indonesia #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb #DataBreach #ThreatIntelligence #InfoSec

🇷🇺 A document allegedly linked to Russian intelligence and counterintelligence operations concerning Ukraine has surfaced on a cybercrime forum.  The leaked material is being advertised as an internal document related to “measures to counter military, scientific, and technical intelligence of Ukraine’s special services.”  While the authenticity of the document remains unverified, this type of material could potentially provide insight into: • Counterintelligence methodologies   • Operational security procedures   • Intelligence defense strategies   • Information protection frameworks   • Military and technical intelligence priorities   • Internal bureaucratic or strategic processes    State-linked or politically themed document leaks often attract significant attention within underground communities due to their potential intelligence and propaganda value.  Such leaks may also be leveraged for: • Psychological operations (PSYOPs)   • Narrative shaping campaigns   • Information warfare   • Disinformation amplification   • Geopolitical influence operations   • OSINT exploitation and analysis    Researchers and analysts should approach politically charged leaks with caution, as underground forums frequently contain: • Fabricated documents   • Altered or selectively edited materials   • Repackaged public documents   • Propaganda-driven releases   • Contextually manipulated information    This incident further highlights how cybercrime forums increasingly function not only as criminal marketplaces, but also as distribution channels for geopolitical and information warfare content.  At this time, the authenticity, origin, and operational significance of the alleged document remain unverified.  #DDW #Intelligence #Russia #Ukraine #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb #OSINT #ThreatIntelligence #InfoSec

제공해주신 위협 인텔리전스 데이터를 분석한 결과입니다.

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 GitHub Actions, Nx Console 등 소프트웨어 공급망(Supply Chain)을 직접 타겟팅하여 CI/CD 파이프라인의 자격 증명을 탈취하는 전략을 강화하고 있습니다. 또한, AI 프레임워크(OpenClaw 등)의 취약점을 악용하거나, AI를 활용해 취약점 탐색 및 피싱 메일을 정교화하는 등 공격의 자동화와 지능화가 가속화되고 있습니다. 특히, 취약점 공개 후 실제 악용까지 걸리는 시간이 단축됨에 따라, 패치 적용 전의 Zero-Day 공격에 대한 방어 체계 마련이 시급한 상황입니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **UNC6671 (Mandiant):** Vishing을 결합한 BlackFile Extortion 캠페인을 통해 기업 대상 금전적 갈취 활동 수행.
*   **UNC6692 (Mandiant):** 사회 공학적 기법(Social Engineering)을 활용하여 커스텀 악성코드 제품군을 배포하는 다단계 침입 캠페인 수행.
*   **Kimsuky (Kaspersky):** PebbleDash 기반 도구를 사용하여 AppleSeed 악성코드 클러스터와 연계된 정보 탈취 캠페인 수행.
*   **TeamPCP (SANS ISC):** Jenkins 플러그인 등 공급망을 타겟으로 한 지속적인 캠페인 수행.
*   **DieNet (CyberAsia):** 이스라엘 국방부 웹사이트를 타겟으로 한 DDoS 및 사이버 공격 수행.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **데일리시큐:** 과기정통부, 10월 정보통신망법 개정 시행을 앞두고 '침해사고 조사 심의위원회' 사전 가동.
*   **AhnLab ASEC:** 국내 의료 초음파 장비 제조 기업의 데이터 유출 주장 및 4월 인포스틸러 동향 보고서 발행.
*   **이스트시큐리티:** 국세청 및 금융기관을 사칭한 PhaaS(Phishing-as-a-Service) 기반의 기업 타겟 피싱 메일 공격 포착.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
1.  **CVE-2026-8153:** Universal Robots PolyScope 5, OS command injection, CVSS 9.8 (악용 중)
